title: Academy content
description: Frontmatter contract for an academy.conduction.nl entry. Each MDX file under sites/academy/content/ has frontmatter that conforms to this schema. Translations live in sites/academy/i18n/<locale>/docusaurus-plugin-content-blog/ and conform to the same schema, with content allowed to diverge per language.

slug.description: URL slug. Optional; Docusaurus derives from filename when omitted. Translations of the same post must share the same slug so URLs match across locales.

title.description: Sentence-case title. Used as the card title and the h1 on the detail page.

contentType.description: Single-pick content type. Drives the chip colour and the filter row on the landing.

authors.description: Author keys. Resolved against sites/academy/content/authors.yml. Multiple authors render as 'A & B' in bylines.

date.description: Publish date in ISO 8601 (YYYY-MM-DD). Used for sort order and the byline.

tags.description: Topical tags shown next to the content-type chip. Free-form for now; can be promoted to a controlled vocabulary later.

apps.description: App slugs from the shared apps-registry (docusaurus-preset/src/data/apps-registry.js). Drives the product filter row on the academy landing and the cross-link block on the post detail page. Multiple apps allowed when a post covers a pipeline that spans products. Optional; posts that aren't about a specific app omit it.

audience.description: Target audiences for this entry, from the three tiers defined in design-system/CLAUDE.md and preview/identity/audience.html. `mkb` = MKB-beslisser / IT-lead (primary), `government` = Overheid-beslisser (secondary), `developer` = Developer / integrator (tertiary). One or more values. A post is shown when ANY of its audiences matches the active filter. List the primary audience first by convention.

module.description: Optional module slug. Groups multiple entries into an ordered learning path (e.g. `deskdesk-tutorial` for the four-part build series). Use the same slug across every entry in the module. Combine with `modulePosition` to define the order. Modules can mix content types: a module may include guides, tutorials, and a closing webinar.

modulePosition.description: Position of this entry inside its `module`, starting at 1. Required when `module` is set so the TOC / next-prev navigation can render in a deterministic order. Gaps are allowed (1, 2, 4) for in-progress modules.

moduleTitle.description: Optional human-readable title for the module. Set this on the entry at `modulePosition: 1` (the canonical source); other parts may omit it. Used by the composite <ModuleCard/> on the academy landing and the <ModulePage/> wrapper for /academy/modules/{slug}. Falls back to a kebab→sentence-case render of the `module` slug when absent.

moduleLede.description: Optional one- or two-sentence summary of the module. Set this on the entry at `modulePosition: 1` (the canonical source). Used as the lede on the composite <ModuleCard/> and as the hero copy on the module index page. Falls back to the first part's `summary` when absent.

heroImage.description: Path to the hero image used in <ContentDetailHero/>. Relative to the post folder.

summary.description: One-line summary. Shown under the title on cards and as the lede on the detail page. Keep under 160 characters.

durationMinutes.description: Read or watch time in minutes. Renders as 'N min read' (blog/guide/case-study/tutorial) or 'N min watch' (webinar).

videoUrl.description: Webinar video URL. Required for contentType=webinar; ignored elsewhere.

thumbnailTone.description: Optional tone override for the card thumbnail panel. Defaults are derived from contentType so editors normally do not set this.
